items.title: Anthropic releases Claude Opus 4.7, concedes it trails unreleased Mythos
items.author: www.axios.com
items.snippet: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.7 on April 16, 2026, as its most capable publicly available model to date. The release brings a 13% lift on coding benchmarks, 3x more production tasks resolved, and significantly improved multimodal understanding. Key new features include high-resolution image support (up to 2,576 pixels / 3.75 megapixels — more than 3x the previous limit), sharper instruction following, more reliable long-running agent work, new effort controls (including a new 'xhigh' effort l

items.title: Claude API Release Notes Overview — Anthropic Platform Docs
items.author: platform.claude.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has been active with API and model updates through early 2026. Major April 2026 highlights include the public beta launch of Claude Managed Agents — a fully managed agent harness offering secure sandboxing, built-in tools, and server-sent event streaming (requires the `managed-agents-2026-04-01` beta header). Anthropic also launched the `ant` CLI, a command-line client for the Claude API with native Claude Code integration and YAML-based API resource versioning. The max_tokens cap on t

items.title: Anthropic Rebuilds Claude Code Desktop App Around Parallel Sessions — MacRumors
items.author: www.macrumors.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has been active on Twitter (@AnthropicAI) in April 2026 with several major announcements. The biggest news is the release of Claude Opus 4.7, described as Anthropic's most powerful generally available model, featuring improved software engineering capabilities and built-in cybersecurity safeguards. Alongside this, Anthropic unveiled Project Glasswing and a preview of Claude Mythos — an advanced AI that autonomously identified and exploited a 17-year-old remote code execution vulnerabil

items.title: Automated Weak-to-Strong Researcher
items.author: alignment.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has published several notable research papers and reports in early 2026. On the alignment and safety front, researchers published 'Automated Weak-to-Strong Researcher,' where autonomous AI agents proposed ideas, ran experiments, and iterated on the problem of training a strong model using only a weaker model's supervision — outperforming human researchers. Separately, 'The Hot Mess of AI: How Does Misalignment Scale with Model Intelligence and Task Complexity?' decomposed frontier reas
